detection_signals: The lowest-yielding vineyard always makes the best wine
If the yield is high, the quality must be low
Reducing yield infinitely will infinitely improve wine quality
why_incorrect: While lower yield can concentrate sugars per grape, there is no absolute relationship guaranteeing better wine. Different varieties and terroirs have different optimal yield points.
misconception: Lower yield always produces more concentrated and higher-quality wine.
corrected_understanding: Lower yield can enhance concentration, but there is no absolute yield-to-quality relationship. Optimal yield varies by terroir, variety, and vintage.
